4.1. Intertwiners

Definition 3. intertwines two matrices and if

(15)

Proposition 3 (For fun). Nonzero intertwiner common eigenvalue Invertible intertwiner same Jordan canonical form up to change of basis.

When representations are completely reducible, Schur’s lemma tells us there only exists a nonzero intertwiner if there is some irrep of in . It is full rank if irreps of occur in with at least the same multiplicities.

For the condition to be dimensionally well-typed, we require and must be square matrices (possibly of different dimensions) for there to exist . In particular, if is and is then , and are .

To solve for such that , when can write it as for some larger matrix . The Kronecker product and sum make this conversion possible.

Definition 4 (Kronecker product and sum). For and , define the Kronecker product as

(16)

For square matrices and , define the Kronecker sum as

(17)

Einsum notation

In einsum notation, the Kronecker product is

# A = (a,b) and B = (c,d) 
np.kron(A, B) == np.einsum('ab, cd-> acbd', A, B).reshape((a*c, b*d))

Justification of algorithm

Proposition 4. The Kronecker product satisfies:

(19)

(20)

We now have an algorithm to find similarity transforms via solving for the null space of a big matrix. We can solve for equivaraint maps by solving the multiple pair case, one pair for each group element.
